obligation_text: Before making a high-risk AI system available on the market, distributors shall verify that the high-risk AI system bears the required CE marking, that it is accompanied by the required EU declaration of conformity and instructions for use, and that the provider and the importer have complied with their respective obligations.
scoring_text: Knowledge of AI system distribution compliance, including CE marking verification, documentation compliance checking, and supply chain compliance verification for provider and importer obligations.
_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Knowledge of AI system distribution compliance, including CE marking verification, documentation compliance checking, and supply chain compliance verification for provider and importer obligations.
reference_document_element_description: Knowledge of AI regulatory compliance requirements, including EU AI Act conformity assessment obligations, prohibited use categories, and documentation requirements for high-risk AI systems.
comments: EU AI Act Art. 24 (obligation) superset of WIDAI AG domain. AG-K-008 is the strongest match (STS: 0.633, cosine: 0.731). Secondary: SP-K-006 (SP, cosine: 0.714), RC-A-005 (RC, cosine: 0.686).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression. Primary method for all elements. Scores the competency_implication text (workforce competency interpretation of the regulatory requirement) against WIDAI KSA statements.
primary_method.significance: STS 6.3/10 (raw 0.633). Shared vocabulary: [ai, compliance, documentation, knowledge, obligations]. EU AI Act competency implication terms [ce, chain, checking, distribution, importer, marking] vs WIDAI terms [act, assessment, categories, conformity, eu, high] define the semantic distance.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.891 (P=0.891, R=0.891). Balanced P/R — the 23-word competency implication and 23-word WIDAI KSA cover each other's token-level semantic content symmetrically.

nli_cross_encoder.description: Natural Language Inference classification. NLI entailment is systematically low for competency→competency pairs (mean ~0.007) because NLI measures strict logical implication, not semantic relatedness. Primary diagnostic value is in detecting contradiction.
nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (99.9%). Standard result for cross-framework competency mapping — the EU AI Act element and WIDAI KSA describe overlapping competencies without strict logical implication.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.731 > STS 0.633 (gap +0.097). Independent encoding finds more similarity than cross-attention. Shared surface terms [ai, compliance, documentation, knowledge, obligations] inflate the bi-encoder, but cross-attention detects semantic divergence beneath the vocabulary overlap.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 29.0%. Shared tokens: [ai, compliance, documentation, knowledge, obligations]. EU AI Act-only: [ce, chain, checking, distribution, importer, marking]. WIDAI-only: [act, assessment, categories, conformity, eu, high].
